title: HTTP Caching, Freshness, Validators, Vary, and Invalidation · Go Client Systems · Runtime Recall
summary: A security reviewer reconstructing an expired incident handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for http caching, freshness, validators, vary, and invalidation, producing spec-aware cache decision notebook. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Go 1.26 net/http spec-aware cache decision notebook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-…
objectives: Distinguish private, shared, gateway, browser, and application caches plus who may reuse stored responses. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Compute freshness from explicit directives, dates, age, and response context rather than wall-clock intuition. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Use ETag and Last-Modified validators with If-None-Match or If-Modified-Since and interpret 304 correctly. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Stale Revalidation Invalidation
steps.instruction:
review case — Go security handoff client stores a response with max-age=30, ETag "rev-3", Age:3, and Vary: Accept-Language. A user-specific request arrives stale; compute reuse, revalidation, 304 metadata merge, cache key, and privacy policy. Competency probe: Choose revalidation, stale serving, explicit invalidation, or bypass while preserving correctness during failure. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Inspect the Stale Revalidation Invalidation trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Stale Revalidation Invalidation.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose revalidation, stale serving, explicit invalidation, or bypass while preserving correctness during failure. demonstrate the result through go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, roundtripper, and httptest evidence. For Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Stale Revalidation Invalidation, keep Stale Revalidation Invalidation connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
